methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: planqc

data.narrative:
Nu Quantum is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Carmen Palacios-Berraquero that has raised $60M to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable approach. Using total raised ($60M) as a proxy for post-money valuation and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage step-ups yields an implied valuation of $120M. The peer-median approach is not applicable: while 7 quantum computing peers exist in the broader dataset, filtering for same-stage or adjacent-stage companies within similar valuation tiers produced fewer than 3 datapoints, insufficient for a reliable median calculation. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce material measurement error.

Analysis supports a valuation range of $102M to $138M, with a median of $120M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the absence of sufficient narrow-stage peer comparables, which limits cross-validation of the step-up multiplier; actual post-money valuation from the Series A round, if available, would strengthen confidence in the base input; and quantum computing's nascent commercialization profile, which creates structural difficulty in traditional multiple-based approaches.
